findings.title: conflict-copy strategy entirely absent from syncEngine download tests
findings.why_it_matters: The _applyConflictCopy branch in _applyRecord has no unit test coverage. The file claims conflict-copy coverage in its header comment but the mock registry sets notes to 'lww'. A regression in the integration between _applyRecord dispatch and _applyConflictCopy would be invisible.
findings.suggested_fix: Add describe('conflict-copy strategy') with: remote wins+content differs→conflictCopy snapshot, remote wins+same content→LWW, local wins→LWW, new record→plain put.
evidence: Line 166 of syncEngine.download.test.ts: conflictStrategy: 'lww' — notes table mock does not exercise conflict-copy
No describe block or test case mentioning 'conflict-copy' or 'conflictCopy' in the 524-line test file
_applyConflictCopy at syncEngine.ts:510 is exercised only by conflictResolvers.test.ts at the pure-function level, not the integration dispatch level

findings.title: E2E Test 5 does not verify content replaced on Use Other Version
findings.why_it_matters: Test 5 only checks that the badge disappears. It does not verify that the note content was actually replaced with conflictCopy.content (LOSING_CONTENT). If handleUseOtherVersion had a bug where it cleared conflictCopy but did not replace the content, Test 5 would still pass.
findings.suggested_fix: After badge disappears, verify note content shows the losing version text using the Dexie check pattern (as in Test 6) or by checking the visible note text in the DOM.
evidence: Test 5 line 146: asserts `dialog not visible` and line 150: asserts `badge not visible` — no content assertion
handleUseOtherVersion in NoteConflictDialog.tsx lines 213-214 spreads `content: note.conflictCopy.content, tags: note.conflictCopy.tags` — these could be separately tested
Test 6 tests Dexie state for Keep Current but there is no Dexie-state test for Use Other Version

findings.title: No E2E test for notes list view (Notes.tsx) conflict dialog flow
findings.why_it_matters: The E2E tests target the Notes.tsx list view (via /notes page). The conflict badge in Notes.tsx is a separate code path from NoteCard.tsx (different button implementation, different state management with conflictNoteId). The tests use `getByRole('button', { name: /sync conflict/i })` which could match either. If Notes.tsx has a bug where the badge triggers the wrong note's dialog, the tests would not catch it (only one note with a conflict is seeded).
findings.suggested_fix: Seed two notes with conflicts and verify the correct note's conflictCopy content appears in the dialog after clicking a specific badge.
evidence: Notes.tsx lines 386-396: conflict badge in VirtualizedList view with onClick={() => setConflictNoteId(item.note.id)}
NoteCard.tsx lines 88-96: separate conflict badge with onClick={() => setShowConflictDialog(true)}
Tests always seed exactly one conflict note, so multiple-badge disambiguation is never tested

residual_risks: conflictResolvers.test.ts covers applyConflictCopy well but does not test the case where local already has a non-null conflictCopy (repeat conflict scenario)
No test verifies that conflictSourceId is set correctly on the resolved record
testing_gaps: E2E Test 5: no content assertion for Use Other Version
No test for repeat-conflict (local.conflictCopy already set when new sync arrives)
No test for conflictSourceId being cleared to null after resolution
